content.text:
All 7 files are in place. Here's a summary of what was created:

### `extensions/pulseboard/` — New PulseBoard channel plugin

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Package metadata with `@openclaw/pulseboard` name, `"module"` type, and `openclaw` block defining channel id/label/selectionLabel/docsPath/blurb |
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin manifest with `id: "pulseboard"`, `channels: ["pulseboard"]`, and minimal `configSchema` |
| `src/types.ts` | Three interfaces: `PulseBoardAccount` (boardUrl, apiToken, username), `PulseBoardConfig` (accounts record), `PulseBoardMessage` (id, boardId, authorId, content, timestamp) |
| `src/runtime.ts` | Runtime store via `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>` exporting `getPulseBoardRuntime` and `setPulseBoardRuntime` |
| `src/channel.ts` | Channel plugin built with `createChatChannelPlugin()` — includes meta, capabilities (direct+group, no media), config/setup/gateway/messaging stubs, and outbound adapter with `deliveryMode: "direct"`, `textChunkLimit: 4000`, and `sendText` returning `attachChannelToResult("pulseboard", ...)` |
| `index.ts` | Main entry using `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with id/name/description/plugin/setRuntime |
| `setup-entry.ts` | Setup entry using `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)` |

**One design note:** The requirements mentioned using `getChatChannelMeta` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`, but that function only resolves metadata for built-in core channels (telegram, discord, irc, etc.). Extension channels define their `meta` object inline — this is the pattern used by all extension plugins (e.g., Nostr). The PulseBoard plugin follows that correct extension pattern with inline meta.

result:
All 7 files are in place. Here's a summary of what was created:

### `extensions/pulseboard/` — New PulseBoard channel plugin

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Package metadata with `@openclaw/pulseboard` name, `"module"` type, and `openclaw` block defining channel id/label/selectionLabel/docsPath/blurb |
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin manifest with `id: "pulseboard"`, `channels: ["pulseboard"]`, and minimal `configSchema` |
| `src/types.ts` | Three interfaces: `PulseBoardAccount` (boardUrl, apiToken, username), `PulseBoardConfig` (accounts record), `PulseBoardMessage` (id, boardId, authorId, content, timestamp) |
| `src/runtime.ts` | Runtime store via `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>` exporting `getPulseBoardRuntime` and `setPulseBoardRuntime` |
| `src/channel.ts` | Channel plugin built with `createChatChannelPlugin()` — includes meta, capabilities (direct+group, no media), config/setup/gateway/messaging stubs, and outbound adapter with `deliveryMode: "direct"`, `textChunkLimit: 4000`, and `sendText` returning `attachChannelToResult("pulseboard", ...)` |
| `index.ts` | Main entry using `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with id/name/description/plugin/setRuntime |
| `setup-entry.ts` | Setup entry using `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)` |

**One design note:** The requirements mentioned using `getChatChannelMeta` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`, but that function only resolves metadata for built-in core channels (telegram, discord, irc, etc.). Extension channels define their `meta` object inline — this is the pattern used by all extension plugins (e.g., Nostr). The PulseBoard plugin follows that correct extension pattern with inline meta.
